title: 10 best herbal creams for itchy scalp

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, reduce swelling and itching associated with skin irritation, allowing for a soothing relief from itchiness.

Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage the scalp and cause inflammation, promoting healthy skin cells and reducing oxidative stress that may contribute to itchy scalps.

Hydrating properties: Aloe vera's moisturizing compounds help lock in moisture, soothing dryness and irritation, and calming itchiness by providing a protective barrier against environmental irritants.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with itchy scalp because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to calm irritation and reduce redness on the scalp. Additionally, aloe vera creams can help to lock in moisture, relieving dryness and itchiness. It can also promote a healthy scalp environment, allowing hair to grow strong and healthy while minimizing the occurrence of itchy scalp conditions.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: They help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and itching associated with conditions like dandruff and eczema.
Saponins: These natural compounds have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help control the growth of yeast and fungi that can contribute to scalp irritation and itchiness.

Vitamins and minerals (especially Vitamin E and Zinc): They possess antioxidant properties that help protect the scalp from damage, promote skin cell regeneration, and reduce inflammation, ultimately alleviating itchiness and promoting a healthy scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe vera for skincare products, including creams for itchy scalp, due to their high content of soothing and moisturizing compounds like aloe-emodin and aloin.
Gel from the Leaves: Specifically, the clear gel extracted from the leaves is used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm an itchy scalp.
Aloe Vera Extract from the Leaves: The extract from the leaves is also used, as it contains a high concentration of active compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy scalp skin.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20 ounces of aloe vera gel from 3 to 4 aloe vera leaves using a knife or spoon.
Combine 1/4 cup of distilled water with 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 to 15 minutes or until the beeswax melts.
Add 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the melted beeswax mixture.
Pour the aloe vera gel into the saucepan with the melted mixture and stir until well combined.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Stinging nettle creams contain compounds that reduce inflammation, which can soothe and calm an itchy scalp, providing relief from itching and irritation.

Antioxidant property: Antioxidants present in stinging nettle creams help to neutralize free radicals and protect the skin from oxidative damage, reducing redness and inflammation associated with an itchy scalp.

Antihistamine property: Stinging nettle creams contain histamine-releasing compounds that can help reduce histamine levels in the body, which is often responsible for itching, thereby providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb contains compounds like flavonoids and saponins that help to calm and reduce inflammation on the scalp, thereby alleviating itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, stinging nettle creams often contain other ingredients like tea tree oil and aloe vera that further enhance their soothing and moisturizing effects, making them an effective natural remedy for itchy scalp conditions.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (specifically, Histamine and other alkaloid compounds): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the itching and inflammation caused by an itchy scalp.
Phenolic compounds (specifically, Quercetin and Isoquercitrin): These flavonoids have potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects, which can help alleviate itching and soothe the scalp.

Steroids (specifically, Stigmasterol and other triterpenoids): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation, itching, and irritation on the scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Urtica dioica are commonly used to make creams for an itchy scalp due to their high content of anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.
Roots: The roots of Urtica dioica are used to make creams for an itchy scalp because they contain compounds that help to soothe and calm irritated skin.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica are also used to make creams for an itchy scalp, as they possess anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce itching and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of fresh urtica dioica leaves and 200ml of oil with a melting point around 35°c for infusing.
Infuse the urtica dioica leaves in the oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix the infused oil with 10g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c to 70°c for 10-15 minutes until it reaches a creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm cream's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and soothe itching caused by oxidative stress and damage to the skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm cream, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with itchy scalp conditions like psoriasis and eczema.

Antiseptic and antifungal properties: Lemon balm's antiseptic and antifungal properties help to prevent infection and reduce the risk of fungal or bacterial overgrowth on the scalp, which can exacerbate itching and irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with itchy scalp because of its soothing and antifungal properties. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory effects that calm irritated skin and reduce redness. Additionally, Melissa officinalis has antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal infections, such as dandruff and ringworm, that can cause itchy scalp. By using Melissa officinalis creams, individuals can experience relief from itchy scalp and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene, linalool has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and calm the itchy scalp, reducing irritation and redness.
Roscavin: A phenolic compound, roscavin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce inflammation and itching associated with scalp conditions, promoting healthy scalp skin.
Limonene: A terpene, limonene has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat fungal and bacterial infections causing scalp itchiness, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing itchy scalps.
Roots: The roots contain bioactive compounds that help to calm and reduce inflammation, providing relief from itchy scalp conditions.
Stems: The stems of Melissa officinalis have been used in traditional medicine to treat skin and scalp issues, including itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 50g of melissa officinalis leaves in 500ml of jojoba oil for 2 hours under low heat.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Combine the strained oil with 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under gentle heat for 10 minutes stirring occasionally until beeswax and shea butter melt.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before transferring it to a storage container.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that reduce inflammation and itching in the scalp, providing relief from irritations such as dandruff and psoriasis.

Antimicrobial properties: The cream's antimicrobial activity helps to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness and flakiness on the scalp, promoting a healthy and balanced environment for hair growth.

Soothing and emollient properties: Pot marigold extract has been shown to possess moisturizing and calming effects on the skin, helping to soothe and calm an itchy scalp, reduce redness, and leave the hair feeling soft and silky.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream calms and soothes the skin, reducing irritation and redness associated with itchy scalps. The plant's natural antifungal and antibacterial properties help combat scalp infections and conditions such as dandruff and seborrheic dermatitis, promoting a healthy scalp environment and relieving itchy sensations. Regular use of Calendula officinalis cream can lead to a reduction in itchy scalp symptoms and promote a balanced scalp ecosystem.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help with itchy scalp by reducing inflammation and fighting off fungal and bacterial infections that can cause irritation and itchiness.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): They help with itchy scalp by reducing inflammation and acting as an antioxidant to protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchiness and irritation.

Triterpenoids (specifically, oleanolic acid): They help with itchy scalp by reducing inflammation and acting as an antifungal agent to combat infections that can cause itchiness and irritation on the scalp.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing itchy scalps.
Leaves: Although less commonly used, they still contain some of the plant's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate itchy scalp symptoms.
Seeds: They are often overlooked but are known to contain some of the plant's medicinal properties, which can contribute to the effectiveness of creams for itchy scalps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning to ensure optimal potency.
Infuse 1 cup of flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter in a double boiler.
Pour the cooled mixture into small tin containers and label them for storage and use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sesame cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesamin and sesamolin, which can reduce inflammation and soothe irritation on the scalp, providing relief from itching.
Antifungal and antibacterial: The cream's antimicrobial properties can help combat fungal and bacterial infections that may be contributing to an itchy scalp, such as dandruff, ringworm, or eczema.

Moisturizing and emollient: Sesame oil, a main ingredient in sesame creams, is rich in antioxidants and has moisturizing and emollient properties, which can help lock in moisture, calm dryness, and reduce itching associated with flaky or irritated skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesamum indicum, also known as sesame, creams helps with itchy scalp because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream, rich in sesamin and sesamolin, helps to calm the skin and reduce inflammation, providing relief from itchy scalp conditions such as dandruff and eczema. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of the cream help to protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy hair growth and a smooth, itch-free scalp. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe and calm the scalp.
remedy_constituents: Fatty acids (especially Oleic acid): Oleic acid helps soothe and calm the itchy scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy skin barrier.
Sesamin: Sesamin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce redness and itchiness of the scalp by fighting off free radicals and soothing irritated skin.

Sesquiterpenes (including Sesquilin): Sesquiterpenes, such as Sesquilin, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help to combat scalp infections and reduce itchiness by controlling the growth of fungal and bacterial pathogens.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Sesame seeds are commonly used to make creams for itchy scalp due to their anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties.
Leaves: Sesame leaves are used to make creams for itchy scalp due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties that help soothe and calm the scalp.
Seeds: Sesame seeds are also used to make creams for itchy scalp due to their omega-3 fatty acids, which help reduce inflammation and promote healthy scalp and hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of sesamum indicum flowers at dawn to ensure optimal potency retention.
Dry the flowers at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 200g of dried sesamum indicum flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 50g of the ground powder with 100g of cold-pressed coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously for 10 minutes and allow it to cool before transferring to a container.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Many herbal teas, such as chamomile and calendula, contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm itchy scalps by reducing inflammation and redness.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Teas like tea tree and peppermint have natural antimicrobial agents that help combat scalp infections, such as dandruff and psoriasis, which can cause itchiness and irritation.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal teas rich in antioxidants, like green tea and ginkgo biloba, help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin cell growth to alleviate itchiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with itchy scalp because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants present in Camellia sinensis help to calm and reduce inflammation on the scalp, providing instant relief from itchiness and irritation. The natural ingredients in these creams also help to balance the pH of the scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness, and promoting a healthy scalp environment. Regular use can lead to a reduction in itchiness and a healthier scalp.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and itching associated with an itchy scalp.
Theaflavins: These flavonoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help soothe the scalp, reduce inflammation, and alleviate itchy sensations.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has potent anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic properties, which help reduce itching, inflammation, and redness caused by allergic reactions or skin irritations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp.
Stems: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with an itchy scalp.
Barks: They possess astringent properties that can help to reduce irritation and itching on the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 10 grams of dried camellia sinensis leaves in 250ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a bowl to separate solids from liquid.
Combine 50ml of the camellia sinensis liquid with 100g of distilled water and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat while stirring occasionally for 10-15 minutes until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before whipping it with a hand mixer until light and fluffy.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenoids and flavonoids, help reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by skin irritations on the scalp.
Antiparasitic: Wormwood's antiparasitic properties can help eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchy scalps, including conditions like ringworm and dandruff.
Antioxidant: Wormwood's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to scalp irritation, promoting healthy skin and reducing inflammation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium, also known as wormwood, creams helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties. The plant's essential oils, particularly thujone, have been shown to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and itching. The antifungal properties of wormwood also help control scalp infections, such as dandruff and fungal dermatitis, which can cause itchiness. Additionally, wormwood's astringent properties help balance the scalp's pH, creating an environment that promotes healthy hair growth and reduces itchiness.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: A sesquiterpene ketone that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and irritation that cause an itchy scalp.
Lupulin: A sesquiterpene that has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that contribute to scalp itchiness.
Absinthol (Artabsin): A sesquiterpene lactone that has anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, which can help soothe and calm the itchy scalp, reducing the urge to scratch.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm an itchy scalp.
Stems: The stems are used for their astringent properties, which can help reduce inflammation and itchiness on the scalp.
Buds: The buds are used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce irritation and soothe an itchy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers at peak potency for maximum oil yield.
Infuse 50g of the dried plant material in 500ml of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material.
Mix 200g of the infused oil with 100g of beeswax and 50g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Whip the cooled mixture to a smooth consistency before transferring it to a storage container.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as triterpenoids, can soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing itchiness and inflammation.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in the cream, like polyphenols, can help combat fungal and bacterial infections that may contribute to an itchy scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in the cream, such as flavonoids, can neutralize free radicals that cause oxidative stress, which can exacerbate itchiness and scaliness on the scalp.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe irritated skin. The natural compounds present in Echinacea angustifolia, such as alkylamides and polyacetylenes, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing. These creams can also help to balance the scalp's pH, creating a healthy environment for hair growth and reducing the occurrence of itchy scalp conditions. This makes Echinacea angustifolia creams a popular natural remedy for itchy scalp issues.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, specifically echinacoside, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation associated with itchy scalp, providing relief from discomfort.
Alkaloids: Alkaloids in Echinacea angustifolia, such as echinamine and echinacenine, have been shown to have antipruritic effects, which help to soothe and calm itchy scalp conditions.

Tannins: Tannins in Echinacea angustifolia help to reduce itching and inflammation by constricting blood vessels and reducing the release of histamine, a chemical mediator involved in allergic reactions and itching.
remedy_parts: Roots: High in isobutyl amide, a compound that may help soothe itchy scalps.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for itchy scalp relief.
Buds: Contain compounds like alkylamides that may help reduce inflammation and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of dried echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall when the plants are mature.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 150f for 2-3 hours or until completely dry.
Grind the dried echinacea roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered echinacea with 1 cup of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Stir the mixture until it thickens and forms a smooth, creamy texture over 10-15 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Licorice root's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and liquiritin, soothe and calm irritated skin on the scalp, reducing inflammation and itchiness.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal licorice creams contain antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate itching and flaking.

Hormone-balancing effects: Licorice root has been shown to have hormone-balancing properties, which may help regulate hormonal imbalances in the scalp that can contribute to itchiness and dryness, such as those experienced during menopause or pregnancy.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, found in licorice root, calms irritation and reduces redness associated with itchy scalps. It also has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help combat scalp infections and balance the scalp's natural pH, leading to a healthier scalp environment. This results in a reduction in itchiness and flakiness, promoting a comfortable and manageable scalp.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and itching in the scalp.
Isoflavones: These plant-derived flavonoids have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the scalp from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing itchiness.
Licoflavonoids: These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps soothe and calm itchy scalps.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and itching on the scalp.
Barks: The barks are used because they have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, including itchy scalps, due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
remedy_recipe: Extract 500g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots using 2.5 liters of 80% ethanol at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Filter the resulting liquid to obtain 1 liter of crude extract which contains 20% glycyrrhizic acid.
Mix 200g of distilled water with the crude extract to obtain a final concentration of 10% glycyrrhizic acid.
Combine 300g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt at 40-50°c.
Add the aqueous glycyrrhizic acid solution to the melted mixture of beeswax and coconut oil while stirring at 60-70°c for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antipruritic property: The cream contains compounds that help to reduce itching and inflammation, providing relief from an itchy scalp. This is achieved through the reduction of histamine release and decreased sensitivity of nerve endings.

Anti-inflammatory property: The herbal extracts in the cream have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling associated with an itchy scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Antimicrobial property: The cream's antimicrobial properties help to combat fungal infections that can cause itching, flaking, and irritation on the scalp, such as dandruff and ringworm.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tilia platyphyllos, also known as broad-leaved lime, creams helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The presence of flavonoids and tannins in the plant helps to calm and reduce inflammation in the scalp, providing relief from itching and irritation. The cream's moisturizing properties also help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing dryness and itchiness. Regular use can lead to a healthier and more balanced scalp, reducing the occurrence of itchy scalp conditions.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Tilia platyphyllos creams have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and inflammation.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Tilia platyphyllos creams have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help prevent infection and reduce inflammation in the scalp, alleviating itchiness and discomfort.

Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids in Tilia platyphyllos creams have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp, leading to a decrease in itchiness and promotion of healthy scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated scalp skin.
Buds: Contain astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and itching on the scalp.
Barks: Have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that aid in healing and soothing itchy scalp skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 bunch of tilia platyphyllos leaves and flowers in the early morning for maximum potency.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves and flowers by air drying them for 7 days.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried tilia platyphyllos in 1 cup of oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter in a heat-proof bowl.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fragrant sumac creams helps with itchy scalp because of its unique blend of herbal properties. The cream's anti-inflammatory agents soothe irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort caused by itchiness. Sumac's antiseptic and antifungal properties help to calm the scalp, preventing infections that can exacerbate itching. Additionally, sumac's natural moisturizing effects lock in hydration, calming dryness and flakiness that can contribute to itchy scalps. Overall, fragrant sumac creams provide a holistic approach to relieving itchiness, promoting a healthy and comfortable scalp.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Sumac cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce redness and swelling associated with an itchy scalp, providing relief from discomfort and itching.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant property of sumac cream helps to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp, leading to itchiness and irritation.

Antimicrobial property: Sumac cream's antimicrobial property helps to combat fungal infections like dandruff, ringworm, and other conditions that can cause itching, flaking, and irritation on the scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Fruits: Contain astringent properties that can help reduce itching and inflammation by tightening skin pores and reducing moisture.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry 20 grams of fresh rhus aromatica leaves and 10 grams of dried rhus aromatica bark for infusion.
Combine the dried herbs in a clean glass jar, add 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil, and let it infuse for 2 weeks at room temperature.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Melt 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over simmering water, then slowly add 100 milliliters of the herbal oil mixture while whisking constantly.
Remove from heat, let cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes before whipping into a creamy consistency, resulting in a smooth and soothing cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with itchy scalp because of its natural antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb has been used for centuries to soothe and calm irritated skin, making it an effective solution for alleviating itchiness and discomfort associated with dandruff, psoriasis, and eczema. When applied topically, lemongrass creams can help reduce flaking, scaling, and inflammation, leaving the scalp feeling cool, calm, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce redness and swelling associated with an itchy scalp.
Antibacterial and antifungal property: Lemongrass's antimicrobial properties can help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness, flaking, and irritation on the scalp.

Soothing and calming property: The essential oils in lemongrass, such as citral and geraniol, have a calming effect on the skin, which can help to reduce itching, irritation, and discomfort associated with an itchy scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm itchy scalp irritation.
Rhizomes: Contain essential oils with antifungal and antibacterial properties, effective in reducing itchiness caused by fungal or bacterial infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and wash them thoroughly in cold water.
Combine the washed leaves with 250ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Heat the mixture using a low heat setting for 30 minutes to extract the essential oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids before adding 2% tea tree oil.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to airtight containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with itchy scalp because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's active ingredients, such as gingerols and shogaols, penetrate deep into the scalp to reduce inflammation and redness, providing quick relief from itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, herbal ginger creams often contain antifungal and antibacterial agents that help control fungal and bacterial infections, further addressing underlying causes of an itchy scalp. This natural approach can bring much-needed relief for individuals dealing with itchy scalps.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal ginger creams contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness, swelling, and itching associated with an itchy scalp.
Antimicrobial property: Ginger's antimicrobial properties can help combat fungal infections, such as dandruff or ringworm, that may cause itchiness and irritation on the scalp.
Analgesic property: Ginger's analgesic effects can provide relief from itching, burning, and stinging sensations, making it a soothing treatment for an itchy scalp.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes help soothe and calm an itchy scalp while reducing redness and irritation.
Roots: With its natural antimicrobial properties, roots can effectively combat fungal and bacterial infections that cause itchiness on the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Grate 2 tablespoons of fresh ginger root into a small saucepan containing 4 cups of distilled water.
Simmer the grated ginger in the water for 20 minutes over low heat until the liquid reduces by half.
Strain the infused ginger liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids.
Combine the cooled infused ginger liquid with 8 ounces of unflavored coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler over medium heat.
Whisk the mixture constantly until it thickens and cools to room temperature, taking approximately 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with itchy scalp because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The curcumin present in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe irritation, providing relief from itching and flaking associated with conditions such as psoriasis, dandruff, and seborrheic dermatitis. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the scalp, reducing dryness and itchiness, while its antifungal properties combat fungal infections that can contribute to an itchy scalp.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, reduce inflammation and itching associated with skin conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, and eczema.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in turmeric cream help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the scalp, leading to itchiness and flaking.

Anti-fungal and antibacterial properties: Turmeric's antifungal and antibacterial compounds inhibit the growth of fungal and bacterial infections on the scalp, which can contribute to itchiness, redness, and flaking.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used to make creams due to their potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm an itchy scalp.
Roots: Turmeric roots are a rich source of curcuminoids, which have natural antihistamine properties that can provide relief from itchiness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 250 grams of raw curcumin powder and mix it with 100 milliliters of cold pressed coconut oil.
Combine the curcumin mixture with 50 grams of beeswax pellets and heat the blend in a double boiler until melted and smooth.
Add 20 drops of peppermint essential oil and stir well to combine, allowing the mixture to cool slightly.
Whip the cooled mixture with an electric mixer for approximately five minutes or until it reaches a creamy consistency similar to butter.
Fill sterile glass jars with the curcuma cream, labeling them with the preparation date and storing in the refrigerator for up to six weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with itchy scalp because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated skin. The anti-inflammatory properties of Ceylon cinnamon work to reduce redness and swelling, while its antiseptic qualities help to eliminate any underlying infections or irritants that may be contributing to the itchiness. Additionally, the moisturizing effects of the cream help to hydrate the scalp, leaving it feeling soft and manageable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ceylon cinnamon's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with an itchy scalp, providing relief from discomfort.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of Ceylon cinnamon help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness, flaking, and dandruff on the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Antioxidant: Ceylon cinnamon's antioxidant property helps protect the scalp from free radical damage, which can contribute to itchiness, dryness, and irritation, while also promoting overall scalp health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like cinnamaldehyde and cinnamic acid, which help soothe itchiness and reduce inflammation on the scalp.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high amount of starch, which helps moisturize and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and flakiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried cinnamomum verum bark, grind into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine 10g of ground cinnamomum verum bark with 100ml of warm coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to infuse the oil with cinnamomum verum properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids.
Mix 20g of beeswax pellets and 50g of shea butter in a separate double boiler, melt until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Evening primrose creams helps with itchy scalp because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The omega-6 fatty acids in evening primrose oil help to reduce inflammation, which can cause itchiness, redness, and flakiness on the scalp. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture, calming dry and itchy skin, while also promoting healthy hair growth. By addressing the underlying causes of itchiness, these creams provide long-lasting relief for an itchy scalp.
remedy_properties: Omega-6 fatty acids: These essential fatty acids help to reduce inflammation and soothe itchiness, providing relief from irritated scalps.
Gamma-linolenic acid (GLA): GLA is a potent anti-inflammatory agent that helps to calm down redness and irritation, reducing the sensation of itchiness.

Antioxidants: Evening primrose oil is rich in antioxidants like vitamin E, which help to neutralize free radicals and protect the scalp from oxidative stress, further contributing to its soothing effects.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid that helps soothe and calm itchy scalps.
Seeds: High in GLA, which reduces inflammation and irritation associated with itchy scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried oenothera biennis flowers and mix with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Combine the mixture in a saucepan with 50 milliliters of water and heat on low for 15 minutes to create a gentle decoction.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding solids and allowing the liquid to cool completely.
Mix one tablespoon of beeswax pellets with 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a separate bowl until well combined, then slowly add the cooled herbal infusion while whisking.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized cream dispenser or tin, let it set at room temperature for 30 minutes before use or storing in the refrigerator for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet briar creams helps with itchy scalp because they contain antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's active ingredients, such as salicylic acid and tea tree oil, work to reduce redness and swelling, while also targeting the underlying fungal or bacterial infections that can cause itchiness. By reducing inflammation and combatting infections, sweet briar creams provide quick and effective relief from itchy scalp symptoms, leaving skin feeling cool, calm, and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in sweet briar creams help to reduce redness and swelling associated with an itchy scalp, providing relief from discomfort and itchiness.
Antipruritic: Sweet briar creams contain antipruritic agents that directly address the itching sensation on the scalp, reducing the urge to scratch and promoting a sense of calm and comfort.

Antifungal and antibacterial: The antimicrobial properties in sweet briar creams help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchy scalp conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, and seborrheic dermatitis, thus preventing the underlying causes of itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins and flavonoids, sweet briar roots help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itchiness and inflammation.
Rhizomes: The underground stems (rhizomes) contain anti-inflammatory compounds that ease scalp discomfort and reduce redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried rosa rubiginosa petals and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar pestle for 2 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of powdered rosa rubiginosa with 30 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl for 5 minutes.
Place the mixture over low heat on a double boiler and simmer for 10-15 minutes until the mixture reaches a temperature of 60°c to 70°c.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it steep for 30 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil, then strain through cheesecloth for 5 minutes.
Add 10 milliliters of beeswax and stir until fully melted and incorporated, then pour the cream into airtight containers and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months with a shelf life of 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. The herb's natural oils also moisturize the scalp, calming dryness and flaking that can contribute to itchiness. Additionally, fennel's antifungal and antibacterial qualities help combat scalp conditions like dandruff and psoriasis, further alleviating itchy sensations. By addressing underlying issues and providing nourishment, herbal fennel creams offer a gentle yet effective solution for relieving an itchy scalp.
remedy_properties: Antipruritic properties: Fennel cream's antipruritic properties help to reduce itching and irritation on the scalp, providing instant relief from discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in fennel cream can soothe redness and swelling caused by conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, or eczema, reducing inflammation and calming the scalp.

Antimicrobial properties: Fennel's antimicrobial agents can help combat fungal and bacterial infections that may be contributing to itchiness, such as ringworm or scalp acne, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated scalps.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain anise oil, which has antipruritic properties that help to reduce itching and irritation on the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fennel seeds weighing approximately 100 grams for future use.
Combine the harvested fennel seeds with 400 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Place the mixture in a cool dark place for eight weeks allowing for slow infusion and extraction.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Mix the extracted oil with 200 milliliters of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of beeswax to create a creamy emulsion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with itchy scalp because of its natural cooling and calming properties. The menthol in peppermint oil has a soothing effect on the skin, reducing inflammation and itchiness caused by conditions such as dandruff, eczema, and psoriasis. As the cream is massaged into the scalp, it calms irritated skin cells, relaxing tense muscles and reducing flakiness. This results in a more comfortable, less itchy scalp, allowing for improved hair growth and overall scalp health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and methanol, help to reduce redness and swelling on the scalp, providing relief from itchiness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in peppermint oil, like beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can cause irritation and inflammation on the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.

Cooling and calming properties: Peppermint oil's cooling effect helps to soothe an itchy scalp by reducing heat and discomfort, while its calming nature can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of scalp itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, peppermint roots help soothe and calm itchy scalps by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
Leaves: The menthol-rich leaves provide a cooling effect that calms itching and irritation, making them ideal for alleviating discomfort caused by an itchy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves mentha x piperita in the early morning mist to ensure peak oil content and potency for up to 2 hours.
Chop the harvested peppermint leaves into small pieces and steep them in boiling distilled water for precisely 10 minutes at a ratio of 1 tablespoon per cup.
Allow the mixture to cool slightly before straining it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container retaining approximately 20% oil content.
Mix 2 tablespoons of cold-pressed coconut oil and 1 teaspoon of beeswax in a small saucepan heating it slowly over low heat until melted and smooth in about 5 minutes.
Gradually add the cooled peppermint infusion to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture, stirring constantly to combine at a temperature not exceeding 180°f for approximately 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange creams helps with itchy scalp because they contain natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's active ingredients, such as bergamot and neroli oil, work to reduce redness and itching by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness that can contribute to itchiness. By addressing these underlying causes, bitter orange creams provide long-lasting relief from an itchy scalp.
remedy_properties: Antipruritic property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in bitter orange, such as limonene and flavanoids, help to reduce itching and alleviate discomfort on the scalp.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial agents in bitter orange, like citral and geraniol, inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that can contribute to an itchy scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Anti-inflammatory and soothing property: Bitter orange creams often contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin on the scalp, reducing redness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which help soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Barks: Contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of citrus x aurantium and dry them thoroughly in a cool dark place for two weeks.
Combine 200 grams of dried citrus peel with 400 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a heatproof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat stirring occasionally until the mixture reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius and infuses for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another heatproof glass container discarding the solids and allowing it to cool.
Add 10 percent vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of beeswax to the infused oil mixture and whip until smooth and creamy.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for itchy scalp are natural remedies that provide relief from scalp irritation and inflammation, helping to soothe and calm the skin. The best herbal creams for itchy scalp include Melaleuca alternifolia, Aloe vera, Urtica dioica, Calendula officinalis, Eucalyptus globulus, Aloe barbadensis, Cymbopogon citratus, Sesamum indicum, Rosmarinus officinalis, and Cassia auriculata. If left untreated, an itchy scalp can lead to anxiety, sleep deprivation, and social isolation, negatively impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy scalp that are treated with herbal creams are skin conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. These creams can also be used to soothe insect bites, minor burns, and skin irritations caused by allergic reactions or poison ivy/oak/sumac. Herbal ingredients such as aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help calm itchy skin, reduce redness, and promote healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with itchy scalp is a blend of neem and tea tree oil cream, followed by a mixture of aloe vera and coconut oil cream. Neem cream helps to reduce inflammation and combat fungal infections, while tea tree oil cream soothes and calms the scalp. Aloe vera and coconut oil cream moisturizes and nourishes the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. This combination provides long-lasting relief from itchiness and flakiness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with itchy scalp because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea tree oil in these creams has been shown to reduce redness and soothe irritation on the scalp. It also has antibacterial properties that help to eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness. Additionally, Melaleuca alternifolia creams can help to balance the pH of the scalp, creating an environment that is less conducive to itchiness and flaking. This makes it a popular natural remedy for an itchy scalp.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy scalp conditions.
Linalool: This terpene has antifungal properties, which can help control fungal infections that can cause itchiness and irritation on the scalp.
Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are rich in essential oils, particularly tea tree oil, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm itchy scalps.
Stems: Stems are a good source of essential oils, which are used to create tea tree oil-based creams that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria causing itchiness.
Barks: Barks of Melaleuca alternifolia contain antimicrobial compounds that help combat fungal and bacterial infections, which are often the cause of itchy scalps.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30g of dried leaves in 250ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Combine the strained oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler until the beeswax and shea butter are fully melted.
Pour the melted mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with itchy scalp because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to reduce inflammation and itching caused by skin conditions such as dandruff and eczema. Aloe vera creams also moisturize the scalp, hydrating dry skin and flakes, and promoting a healthy scalp environment. This natural remedy can provide relief and comfort to itchy scalps, promoting a more comfortable and manageable scalp.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: Help reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, providing relief from itchy scalp and promoting healthy scalp conditions.
Anthraquinones: Act as anti-inflammatory agents, reducing redness and irritation, and also exhibit antimicrobial properties to combat scalp infections that may cause itchiness.

Fatty acids (specifically, oleic and linoleic acids): Possess anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, which help to hydrate the scalp, reduce itchiness, and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe vera, as they contain a high concentration of the gel which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves is used to create creams that help to reduce inflammation and soothe an itchy scalp.
Exudate from Leaves: The exudate, or sap, from the leaves is also used, as it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to calm an itchy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of aloe vera gel and combine it with 50g of coconut oil in a mixing bowl.
Blend the mixture at low speed for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and well combined.
Add 20g of beeswax and 10g of vitamin e oil to the mixture and blend until fully incorporated.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is warm.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and set for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams helps with itchy scalp because they contain stinging nettle extract, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The extract helps to reduce redness and irritation, providing relief from itchy scalp conditions. The anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling and calm the skin, providing long-lasting relief from itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the scalp, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment. This leads to a reduction in itchiness and an overall improvement in scalp health.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: They help by reducing inflammation and soothing irritated skin, which can cause itchiness on the scalp.
Flavonoids: They help by acting as antioxidants, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that may contribute to itchy scalp conditions.
Saponins: They help by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can calm the scalp and reduce itchiness associated with conditions like dandruff or eczema.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the highest concentration of urtic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.
Stems: The stems also contain urtic acid and other compounds that help soothe itchy skin and reduce inflammation.
Roots: The roots of Urtica dioica are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help calm itchy scalps and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 lbs of fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers in the morning on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested leaves and flowers in a single layer at 70°f for 2-3 hours.
Combine 1 cup of dried urtica dioica with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and heat in a double boiler at 100°f for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Store the cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator at 40°f for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and irritation. Calendula's ability to promote wound healing and tissue repair can also help to alleviate itchiness and flakiness associated with conditions like dandruff and eczema. As a result, Calendula creams provide natural relief from itchy scalp, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: Helps by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp, which can contribute to itchy sensations.
Neryl acetate: Helps by possessing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can soothe and calm irritated scalp skin.
Caffeic acid: Helps by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce redness and inflammation associated with itchy scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing itchy scalps and promoting healthy scalp conditions.
Leaves: They contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm and soothe itchy scalps, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Seeds: They are often used in herbal remedies for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm itchy scalps, reducing irritation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30g of dried calendula officinalis flowers in 1 liter of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion and mix 150ml of it with 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 50ml of distilled water and 20g of coconut oil to the beeswax mixture in the double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 10 minutes and then remove it from heat.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to a sterilized container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cooling sensation of eucalyptus globulus oil provides quick relief from itchy scalp conditions. Its decongestant properties help to clear out pores and reduce redness, promoting healthy scalp circulation. The moisturizing properties of the cream soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and itchiness. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus contains a compound called cineole, which has been shown to have antifungal and antibacterial properties, further helping to combat scalp infections and irritations.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This compound helps reduce inflammation and itching associated with itchy scalp by providing an anti-inflammatory effect.
Caryophyllene oxide: This terpenoid has been found to reduce itching and inflammation by acting as an anti-pruritic agent, helping soothe itchy scalp conditions.
Limonene: This compound helps ease itchy scalp conditions by reducing inflammation and exhibiting antifungal properties, which can help combat fungal infections that may be causing itching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high essential oil content, particularly eucalyptol, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that soothe itchy scalps.
Barks: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce itchiness and prevent infection in itchy scalp conditions.
Oils extracted from Leaves: The eucalyptus essential oil extracted from leaves is a primary ingredient in many creams for itchy scalps, providing relief from itchiness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of eucalyptus globulus leaves and dry them in a well-ventilated area for 7 days.
Grind the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine 20g of the eucalyptus globulus powder with 50g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with itchy scalp because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene, which work together to calm and moisturize the scalp. It also has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control the growth of yeast and other microorganisms that can cause itchiness. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment, Aloe barbadensis creams provide relief from itchy scalp conditions.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These glycosidic compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness on the scalp.
Furanocoumarins: These compounds possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to prevent infection and reduce inflammation on the scalp, thus providing relief from itchiness.

Aldehydes: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection on the scalp, thus alleviating itchiness and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of soothing compounds like aloe-emodin, which helps to calm itchy scalp irritations.
Stems: Utilized for their aloe gel content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy scalp conditions.
Barks: Employed in some products due to their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, although less commonly used than leaves and stems.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200 grams of aloe barbadensis gel and transfer it to a blender for processing.
Add 10 grams of vegetable oil and blend the mixture on high speed for 5 minutes.
Gradually add 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of candelilla wax to the blender.
Continue blending the mixture for 2 minutes then heat it in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the double boiler and allow it to cool and thicken before pouring into containers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams helps with itchy scalp because they contain the essential oils of lemongrass, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause itchiness. The antiseptic properties also help to eliminate any underlying infections that may be contributing to the itchiness. By reducing inflammation and eliminating infections, Cymbopogon citratus creams provide relief from itchy scalp conditions, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp.
remedy_constituents: Citral: A naturally occurring terpene that reduces inflammation and soothes the scalp, alleviating itchiness caused by irritation.
Geraniol: A terpene with antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to control fungal infections and reduce redness and itchiness associated with scalp conditions.
Limonene: A terpene with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and prevent infections that cause itchy scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in essential oils, particularly limonene and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and calm the scalp.
Stems: The stems contain a significant amount of citronella oil, which has a cooling effect and helps reduce inflammation, making it an effective treatment for itchy scalp.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Cymbopogon citratus are rich in active compounds like geraniol and citronellal, which have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve itchy scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves and flowers from a trusted source and store them in an airtight container.
Infuse 20g of the harvested cymbopogon citratus in 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil using a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the oil to a clean container.
Combine 2g of beeswax and 5g of coconut oil with the infused oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken then whip it until it forms a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sesamum indicum creams helps with itchy scalp because they contain antifungal and antibacterial properties that soothe and calm the scalp. The natural anti-inflammatory compounds in the cream reduce redness and irritation, providing instant relief from itchiness. Additionally, Sesamum indicum is rich in antioxidants that promote healthy hair growth and prevent damage. The cream's moisturizing properties also help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing itchiness and flakiness, and leaving the scalp feeling smooth and refreshed.
remedy_constituents: Fatty acids: These help in reducing inflammation and soothing the itchy scalp, promoting a calm and healthy scalp environment.
Sesamin: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in alleviating itchiness and reducing irritation on the scalp.
Phenolic acids: These have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in fighting off infections and reducing redness and itchiness on the scalp.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Sesame seeds are often used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm an itchy scalp.
Leaves: Sesame leaves are utilized for their cooling and anti-inflammatory effects, which can provide relief from itchiness and irritation on the scalp.
Seeds (again, due to multiple uses): Sesame seeds are also valued for their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help combat scalp infections that may cause itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of sesamum indicum flowers, carefully picking them in the early morning to retain their potency, and dry them in a warm place for 2 days.
Grind the dried sesamum indicum flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking about 15 minutes.
Mix 50g of the sesamum indicum powder with 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating it gently over low heat for 10 minutes.
Stir in 10g of beeswax, 5g of shea butter, and 5 drops of vitamin e oil into the sesamum indicum and coconut oil mixture, until everything is fully incorporated.
Remove the herbal cream from the double boiler, let it cool slightly, then pour it into a glass jar for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams helps with itchy scalp because they contain the essential oil of rosemary, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause itchiness. The rosemary oil also has natural antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to control the growth of fungi and bacteria that can contribute to scalp conditions such as dandruff and eczema, leading to an itchy scalp. Regular use of these creams can provide long-lasting relief.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: It has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm the itchy scalp, reducing inflammation and preventing infection.
Rosmarinic acid: As an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound, it helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, alleviating itchiness and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Bornyl acetate: As a terpene with antifungal and antibacterial properties, it helps to combat scalp infections and reduce inflammation, leading to relief from itchy scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, which help soothe and calm irritated scalp skin.
Flowers: Contain rosmarinic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce itchiness and inflammation.
Stems: Often used in combination with leaves and flowers, providing a rich source of essential oils that can help alleviate scalp irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves on a dry morning after the dew has evaporated.
Steam distill the leaves to obtain essential oil with a 2-3% yield within 2 hours.
Combine 10g of distilled rosmarinus officinalis essential oil with 90g of beeswax in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over a double boiler for 10-15 minutes to achieve a smooth consistency.
Fill sterilized containers with the cooled cream and seal them for 24 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata creams helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream contains a compound called kaempferol, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin. The natural ingredients in the cream also help to balance the pH of the scalp, creating an environment that is less conducive to fungal and bacterial growth, which can contribute to itchiness. Regular application of Cassia auriculata cream may provide relief from itchy scalp and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_constituents: Auriculatin: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and itching on the scalp.
Auriculatol: It has antimicrobial properties, which help prevent fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchy scalp.
Quercetin: It has anti-allergic properties, which help reduce histamine release and alleviate itching, redness, and inflammation on the scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm an itchy scalp.
Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and promote healthy scalp skin.
Leaves: Also used for their antifungal properties, which help combat fungal infections that may cause an itchy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of cassia auriculata leaves and flowers from a clean source, ensuring freshness and purity.
Dry the harvested cassia auriculata in a low-temperature oven at 60°c for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Steep 20g of dried cassia auriculata in 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler for 30 minutes to infuse.
Strain the cassia auriculata infused coconut oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to separate the oil.
Mix 5g of beeswax with the infused coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler at 70°c for 10 minutes to create a smooth cream.
